name: node-defaults
title: node-defaults
summary: merge single level defaults over a config object
description:
merge single level defaults over a config object
A simple one level options merge utility. This module exports
a function that takes 2 arguments: options and defaults.
When called, it overrides all of undefined properties in
options with the clones of properties defined in defaults.
Sidecases: if called with a falsy options value, options will
be initialized to a new object before being merged onto.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-defaults because merge single level defaults over a config object.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-defaults as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-defaults with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-defaults --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
